mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high PAYEMS periods fall below 0.5% in 40% of cases, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger corporate capacity utilization, leading to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO's predictive power for SPX returns vanishes when 20-day returns in high INDPRO periods show negative or flat performance in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing S&P 500 20-day returns to turn negative as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns becomes positive.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand, creating a positive feedback loop for equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns collapses when their combined predictive power yields a Rank IC below 0.05 or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong demand environment, driving S&P 500 20-day returns positively during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns when the combined signal produces negative returns in 40%+ of cases or the interaction term's coefficient is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5-day decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals imminent monetary easing, creating positive S&P 500 momentum over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 50%+ of instances following FEDFUNDS declines, or the signal's predictive power drops below a 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals tight labor market conditions that drive wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in 50%+ of cases when UNRATE is below 5.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns becomes positive.
